components.value: V, S, M (a patch of white cloth and a patch of black cloth)

area.value: contiguous area consisting of one 10-foot cube/level (S)

description.value: You cause the illumination in the area to seem to flicker erratically, fluctuating between absolute darkness and blinding brightness. The level of light in the area changes at the start of each creatureâs turn, as determined by rolling a percentile die and consulting the following table. d% Illumination level 1â10 Supernatural darkness 11â25 Darkness 26â50 Dim light 51â90 Normal light 91â00 Bright light Even darkvision canât see through supernatural darkness (as deeper darkness ). Bright light affects creatures with light blindness or light sensitivity. For the purpose of superseding its effects with higher-level light or darkness spells, flickering lights counts as a light spell when it increases the ambient light level and a darkness spell when it decreases the ambient light level.
